DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT HER CLAIM IS APPROPIRATE FOR ATTACHMENT OR A SPECIFIC MONETARY AMOUNT. FUTHER PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THE CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT HAS PROVIDED PLAINTIFF WITH 2 OPPORTUNITIES TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT HAS ALSO TOLD PLAINTIFF TO SEEK ASSISTANCE AT THE ACCESS CENTER OF LEGAL AID OFFICE. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF STILL HAS FAILED TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF BAKER, KIMBERLY A CHATMAN, DAYMAN TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY A CORPORATION AND MEMBER OF THE FARMERS INSURANCE GROUP OF COMPANIES (SUED AND SERVED ERRONEOUSLY HEREIN AS "FARMERS INSURANCE CO." SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

calendar.matters:
Court'S Motion To Dismiss Appeal
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: no appearance by appellant. matter submitted.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Peter Mallon of the Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard (334.6880) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's demurrer to the 2nd amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The court has provided plaintiff with 2 opportunities to amend the complaint to plead facts to support a cause of action. The court has also told plaintiff to seek assistance at the ACCESS center of Legal Aid Office. Because plaintiff still has failed to amend the complaint to allege a cause of action, the court does not believe that plaintiff will be able to. Order and judgment signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2011 Continued To Aug-02-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to August 2, 2011. Plaintiff to provide a courtesy copy of opposition to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Continued To Aug-26-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Kimberly A. Baker, 347-0579 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Peter Mallon, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard 288-0340 for deft & moving party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD SPECIFIC FACTS AS TO THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT, HOW IT WAS BREACHED AND WHAT THE DAMAGES WERE AS A RESULT OF THAT BREACH. In addition, the Court stated that plaintiff to file the second amended complaint by 06/03/11. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-29-2011 Continued To Jul-01-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS KIMBERLY A. BAKER AND DAYMAN CHATMAN'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER, WRIT OF ATTACHMENT.

APPEARANCES: Kimberly A. Baker, 347-0579 pltf & moving party in propria persona; Elana M. Gold, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard 334-6880 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Application for right to attach order is Denied. Plaintiff has failed to show that her claim is appropirate for attachment or a specific monetary amount. Futher Plaintiff has failed to establish probable validity of the claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Peter Mallon of the Law Offices of Steven D. Hillyard (334.6880) for defendant and moving party; Kimberly Baker (347.0579) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona. Discussed and the court adopted its ruling as follows: sustained with leave to amend. The court granted plaintiff's request for 30 days leave to amend. Order signed in open court. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
